rankings.title: Subtomogram Averaging of OMV Budding Sites Reveals Conserved Protein Architecture at Membrane Scission
rankings.rationale: Highest novelty (10), strongest groundedness (8). Subtomogram averaging is proven methodology applied to completely novel target (OMV budding). Null result is explicitly informative. Impact is high — resolving the OMV scission mechanism is the field's central question.

rankings.title: Cryo-ET Reveals OMP Barrel Clustering at Polar Budding Sites as Cargo Selection Mechanism
rankings.rationale: Strong measurement transfer (membrane protein array analysis from eukaryotic to bacterial systems). Quantitative comparison of budding vs non-budding sites is a clean experimental design. Moderate resolution concern but density-based analysis is feasible.

rankings.title: Cryo-EM 3D Classification of P. aeruginosa OMV Populations Reveals Discrete Cargo-Defined Subclasses
rankings.rationale: Highest testability — RELION 3D classification is routine. Novelty is high (population-level classification never applied to vesicles). Mechanistic specificity slightly lower because the hypothesis tests for heterogeneity without predicting the mechanism. Impact moderate — classification is a starting point, not a mechanism.

rankings.title: Cryo-EM Classification of OMV Lipid Asymmetry Identifies LPS O-antigen Length as Primary Sorting Signal
rankings.rationale: Weakest survivor. LPS-OMV connection partially known. Corona measurement technically uncertain. No cross-domain creativity bonus (LPS-curvature-OMV connection mostly within microbiology). Quantitative cryo-EM approach adds some value but the concept is not new.

diversity_check.concern: H1, H2, H6 all use variants of cryo-EM averaging — technique-level diversity could be improved in cycle 2

elo_sanity_check.H1_vs_H5: H1 wins (higher novelty and cross-domain creativity)
